Bordeaux
Capital of the world\'s most celebrated wine region, home to over 7,000 châteaux producing legendary Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blends across 60 appellations.
'},"label-13":{popover:'
Beaune
Heart of Burgundy\'s wine trade and seat of the famous Hospices de Beaune auction, gateway to the Côte d\'Or\'s prized Pinot Noir and Chardonnay vineyards.
'},"label-19":{popover:'
Reims
Unofficial capital of Champagne, where centuries-old chalk cellars beneath the city age millions of bottles of the world\'s most famous sparkling wine.
'},"label-21":{popover:'
Strasbourg
Gateway to the Alsace wine route, France\'s oldest, known for aromatic Riesling, Gewurztraminer, and Pinot Gris grown on sun-drenched Vosges foothills.
'},"label-23":{popover:'
Avignon
Historic center of the southern Rhône valley, surrounded by Châteauneuf-du-Pape and Côtes du Rhône vineyards producing bold Grenache-based reds.
'},"label-18":{popover:'
Nantes
Home of Muscadet, France\'s iconic crisp white wine, produced from Melon de Bourgogne grapes in the Loire estuary\'s unique maritime terroir.
